elf: noun. A supernatural being or spirit associated with illness, mischief, and harmful or dangerous magical influence; in later Norse sources,
fell: verb. To make something fall; especially to chop down a tree.
fells: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of fell
felly: noun. The rim of a wooden wheel, supported by the spokes.
feu: noun. Land held in feudal tenure.
fey: adjective. About to die; doomed; on the verge of sudden or violent death.
fleys: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of fley
flue: noun. A pipe or duct that carries gaseous combustion products away from the point of combustion (such as a furnace).
fly: noun. Any insect of the order Diptera; characterized by having two wings (except for some wingless species), also called true flies.
fuel: noun. Substance consumed to provide energy through combustion, or through chemical or nuclear reaction.
full: adjective. Containing the maximum possible amount that can fit in the space available.
fulls: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of full
fully: adverb. In a full manner; without lack or defect; completely, entirely.
fuse: noun. A wick or cord used to convey flame to gunpowder, a bomb, or similar explosive.
leu: noun. The unit of currency of Romania, equal to one hundred bani.
ley: noun. Arable land used temporarily for hay or grazing.
lulu: noun. A remarkable person, object, or idea.
lye: noun. An alkaline liquid made by leaching ashes (usually wood ashes).
lyse: verb. To burst or cut a cell or cell structure; to induce lysis.
sel: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Selkup.
self: pronoun. Himself, herself, itself, themself, themselves; that specific (person mentioned).
sell: verb. To transfer goods or provide services in exchange for money.
slue: verb. To rotate something on an axis.
sly: adjective. Artfully cunning; secretly mischievous; wily.
sue: verb. To file a legal action against someone, generally a non-criminal (civil) action.
sully: verb. To soil or stain; to dirty.
sulu: noun. An all-purpose skirt -like garment worn by men and women in Fiji.
ulu: noun. An all-purpose knife traditionally used by Yup'ik, Inuit, and Aleut women.
useful: adjective. Having a practical or beneficial use.
usefully: adverb. In a useful manner; in a way that achieves something
yell: verb. To shout; holler; make a loud sound with the voice, especially to express intense emotions such as anger or excitement.
yes: particle. Used to show agreement or acceptance.
yule: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Yule.
fyles: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of fyle
lue: verb. To sift using a sieve, particularly in mining tin or silver.
selfly: adjective. Of or pertaining to self or one's own self, personal.
sey: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Secoya.
sull: verb. Of an animal: to stop; to refuse to go on.
ule: noun. A Mexican and Central American tree (Castilla elastica), related to the breadfruit tree, whose milky juice contains caoutchouc.
ull: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ullatan.
yue: noun. An ancient Chinese unit of volume, notionally equivalent to the space occupied by 1200 millet seeds.
